core_mechanism: reconstructing a higher-dimensional object as a warped fibration over a lower-dimensional quotient base along a continuous (circle) symmetry orbit, where the base metric is allowed to change sign so that its singular locus and the singular warp factors cancel, leaving the total space globally regular across that critical surface
brief_reason: The abstract obtains the full solutions as warped compactifications over an 'ambi-polar' (sign-changing) base using a U(1) isometry and shows the complete object is regular around the base's critical/singular surface, i.e. base and warp-factor singularities conspire to smooth out in the total space.
